creator: scheidell@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->feedback

Bruce: 

We are sorry this took so long to get to. 
One of the problems of adding (untested, as in untested by upstream) patches is we don't know their side effects until this is  
put into distibution. 
We would always want to wait until upstream patches their source. 

Also, the quickest way to get a pr committed would be to submit a properly formated patch against the port (not just against  
the source) 

last issue, and I hope this doesn't sound like an excuse. The upstream has released version 4.12 as this writting. 

If you are interested, you can test our 4.12 from the source, 
Make a patch against current (do you use anon cvs? you could use cvs diff) 
If not, copy the original to /var/foomatic-filters 
update your portstree, make clean, rsync -av foomatic-filters/ /var/foomatic-filters 
edit (at least) the ports Makefile, and distinfo 
test port: 
portlint -abt (or if using anon cvs:  portlint -C). fix. 

make package 
pkg_delete (check for leftovers, see pkg-plist) 
pkg_add (just to test the package) 

then create a diff:  diff -ruN (or cvs diff if running anon cvs) 
submit this as an attachment to the current pr, (elm -A {patch} -s "Re: ports/164298: [PATCH] Make print/foomatic-filters not  
eat PS files" bug-followup@FreeBSD.org 

then, current maintainer can test and commit. (current maintainer will get an email when you do this) 

if you are having problems getting this to work, review the porters-handbook on the FreeBSD.org web site, and, join the  
freebsd-ports mailing list and ask for help.
